title: Marrubium litardierei
taxonomy: Marrubium litardierei is classified as a species within the genus Marrubium of the family Lamiaceae. This classification places it in the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and other herbs. The Lamiaceae family is characterized by its aromatic leaves and the production of essential oils, which are often used in cooking and medicine. Marrubium litardierei is a member of the subfamily Lamioideae, which contains a large number of species found in temperate regions of the world. The classification of Marrubium litardierei reflects its relationship to other plants in the Lamiaceae family and its unique characteristics as a species.
